warnings: Absolute pKa is highly sensitive to the G(H+,aq) reference (~1.4 unit shift between Tissandier 1998 and Kelly 2006). Prefer mode='reference' against a known acid in the same family.

Single-conformer result: every species was evaluated at ONE geometry, with no conformational/Boltzmann averaging. For a flexible molecule the Boltzmann-averaged free energy over conformers can differ from a single conformer by several kcal/mol — shifting pKa by >1 unit, E° by >0.1 V, and logP by >0.5. If the molecule has rotatable bonds, run conformer-search first and average, or treat this as a screening estimate tied to the input geometry.

prose:
Aqueous acidity (pKa) of hydrogen cyanide (HCN)

Method provenance:
1. Property: Absolute pKa (298.15 K, 1 atm) for HCN ⇌ CN⁻ + H⁺
2. Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` ⇒ B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RIJK density fitting
3. Geometry provenance: user-supplied protonated (HA) and deprotonated (A⁻) .xyz files; each species geometry fully optimized and frequency-checked at the same B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (ASE BFGS optimiser, fmax ≈ 0.00045 Eh bohr⁻¹; 0 genuine imaginary modes)
4. Solvent/environment: ddCOSMO implicit water (PCM-family continuum), no explicit solvent molecules (gas-phase standard‐state correction of 1.894 kcal mol⁻¹ applied)
5. Charges & multiplicities: HA charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet); A⁻ charge −1, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet); H⁺ reference from Tissandier 1998 (G₍H⁺,aq₎ = −270.28 kcal mol⁻¹)
6. Software: chemkit pka-acidity skill (PySCF v2 backend for DFT, ASE BFGS for optimization)
7. Convergence/caveats: Both species optimized to minima (0 imaginary frequencies). Calculation converged. Warnings returned by engine reproduced verbatim below.

Live log: calculation ran with live output streaming to
  /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/pka-acidity-validation/hydrogen-cyanide/20260626-150418_hydrogen-cyanide_pka_acidity/pka_20260626-151133.out  (still on disk; you can `tail -f` or open for full SCF and frequency details).

Headline result (DFT absolute, ±≈3 pKa units typical uncertainty for this tier):
  pKa₍aq,298 K₎ = 13.824209942916431

Engine warnings:
• Absolute pKa is highly sensitive to the G(H+,aq) reference (~1.4 unit shift between Tissandier 1998 and Kelly 2006). Prefer mode='reference' against a known acid in the same family.
• Single-conformer result: every species was evaluated at ONE geometry, with no conformational/Boltzmann averaging. For a flexible molecule the Boltzmann-averaged free energy over conformers can differ from a single conformer by several kcal/mol — shifting pKa by >1 unit, E° by >0.1 V, and logP by >0.5. If the molecule has rotatable bonds, run conformer-search first and average, or treat this as a screening estimate tied to the input geometry.

The pKa reported is a purely computed estimate (no experimental comparison requested) and should be interpreted within the typical ±3 pKa accuracy envelope of B3LYP/def2-TZVP absolute protocols.
